title: Placus lanuginosus
taxonomy: Placus lanuginosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Placus, which consists of a few species of shrubs and subshrubs. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes species such as forget-me-nots and borage. Placus lanuginosus is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Further classification of the plant includes its order (Boraginales), class (Magnoliopsida), and division (Angiospermae).
